title: Mumbai family deaths: Rat poison ingredient zinc phosphide found in viscera and watermelon sample
content: Further probe is being conducted by the JJ Marg police station, which has recorded statements of kin and neighbours

title: Ally Cong. seeks ‘better coordination over crucial public issues’ with ruling NC
content: “Mutual understanding and cooperation among coalition partners were non-negotiable for smooth governance and effective public welfare. The NC cannot afford to ignore the voice of its alliance partner,” said senior Congress leader and legislator Ghulam Ahmad Mir

title: Students of sealed seminary-cum-school hit streets, demand re-opening in Kashmir’s Shopian
content: Hundreds of students, accompanied by parents, hit the streets in south Kashmir and marched towards the office of the Deputy Commissioner

title: Congress blocks Agra-Mumbai highway in Indore over farmers’ issues
content: The demonstration, held in the Pigdambar area as part of the Congress’s State-wide ‘chakka jam andolan’, led to long queues of vehicles on both sides of the highway

title: Several drugs consignments recovered along Gujarat coast
content: Officials said personnel of the 176 Battalion of the BSF recovered ten packets of the contraband during a patrol operation in the creek region of Kutch on Wednesday

title: Twin blasts: Opposition accuses CM of ‘sedition’; BJP plotting unrest in State, says AAP
content: Jakhar-led team meets DGP, presses for probe into Mann’s statements on explosions, accuses him of giving ‘clean chit’ to Pakistan; Sanjay Singh says attacks part of BJP’s ‘Mission Punjab’

title: Former Rajasthan Minister Mahesh Joshi arrested in Jal Jeevan Mission scam
content: According to the ACB, former Minister Mahesh Joshi is accused of misusing his position as the then PHED Minister and accepting bribes in exchange for tenders

title: Sohrabuddin Sheikh encounter case: Bombay HC dismisses appeals against acquittal of 22 policemen
content: A Division Bench of Chief Justice Shree Chandrashekhar and Justice Gautam Ankhad pronounced the order in open court

title: Facing opposition, Ladakh administration says ‘stakeholders will be onboard on setting up a power corporation’
content: The Ladakh administration’s statement came amid opposition over the proposed formation of a joint venture between the Ladakh Power Development Department and the Rural Electrification Corporation for the power sector in Ladakh

title: West Bengal Governor dissolves State Legislative Assembly
content: The 17th West Bengal Legislative Assembly which was sworn in after the 2021 assembly elections stands dissolved; swearing in of the first BJP government in West Bengal will be held at Brigade Parade Grounds on Saturday

title: Faced harassment from a group of men onboard Delhi-bound flight, alleges MP Mahua Moitra
content: In social media post, she says the group ‘leered’ at her and their actions violate her safety in an aircraft; she tags airline and Minister in the post, says she will file formal complaint later

title: Supreme Court asks if there was a 'proper debate' in Parliament before enacting CEC/EC appointments law
content: SC asks if “ethos” of its 2023 judgment was reflected in parliamentary debate on Bill returning appointment power to the political executive; lawyers note mass suspension of Opposition MPs before debate

title: A year after Operation Sindoor, two schools bear the cost of conflict in Pulwama and Poonch
content: Schools in J&K damaged in Pakistani retaliation recall trauma caused to children following the deaths of students; one school awaits compensation to rebuild infrastructure

title: Health authorities sound caution over mosquito-borne diseases
content: Stress laid on consistent field surveillance and control measures as stagnant water and intermittent rains create breeding grounds for mosquitoes, increasing risk of diseases

title: Seeking a new paradigm for Dravidian exceptionalism
content: At the heart of Vijay’s emphatic win, lies a disenchantment with the smug complacence of rule by both the DMK and, for a decade until 2021, the AIADMK

title: Demand to streamline traffic, ensure commuter safety on Ernakulam Junction, Town station premises
content: The chaotic situation has remained unchanged since redevelopment works began in 2023. Railway sources say the extent of barricaded areas will be scaled down once critical works are completed

title: Canada urged to act against ‘anti-India extremist elements’: MEA
content: Appeal comes against the backdrop of a Canadian intelligence report highlighting the national security threat posed by Khalistani elements

title: Guiding young minds to scale greater heights
content: Reforms ushered in by the Andhra Pradesh government in State-run schools have led to a significant improvement in the recent Class 10 results ,underscoring that committed investment, both in intent and resources, drives better outcomes, finds out P. Sujatha Varma

title: Telangana leads India in human trafficking cases, women form vast majority of victims
content: State accounted for nearly one-fifth of all human trafficking cases registered in the country in 2024.

title: Tamil Nadu seeks postponement of DGP empanelment committee meeting
content: Writes to UPSC saying presence of Chief Secretary in the State is absolutely essential in the next few days

title: Flex boards appear for  leadership change in Dharmadam, Taliparamba in Kannur; CPI(M) criticises campaign
content: A flex board erected at Kurumathur endorsed the appointment of P. Jayarajan as the district secretary and M. Swaraj as the State secretary. The boards were put up in the name of ‘Kurumathur Sakhakkal’

title: Centre calls for steps to safeguard shelter residents from heat
content: A communication from the Union Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs instructs authorities to ensure that shelters had functional fans, water dispensers, and other cooling facilities. It also calls for training shelter staff to identify signs of heat-related illnesses and implement response protocols

title: Nida Khan, key accused in Nashik TCS case, arrested in Maharashtra
content: Arrest comes over 40 days after FIR was registered against her in a rape and sexual exploitation case

title: Off the script on Bengal elections
content: Polls come and go but close encounters with marginalised people stick with a reporter forever and their unanswered questions continue to echo

title: MES to focus on healthcare sector in Kerala
content: Efforts are on to set up new hospitals in Kozhikode, Palakkad, Thrissur, Ernakulam, and Alappuzha, say Muslim Educational Society functionaries

title: Seven infants received at ‘Ammathottil’ in Kozhikode in nine months
content: The district secretary of the Kerala State Council for Child Welfare and the district manager of Sisu Vikas Bhavan receive a mobile app alert as soon as a child is found in the cradle, following which hospital staff inspect the child’s health indicators before adoption procedures are initiated

title: A big question mark over Kerala’s power sector
content: The recent electricity crisis in Kerala has exposed the State’s continued inability to tap the potential of its solar power capacity, which has grown phenomenally in the past decade. Lack of ample systems to store excess power generated during daytime is also a dampener. Tiki Rajwi finds out what is ailing the State’s power sector

title: Action stepped up against alleged cyber extortion
content: Police receive fresh evidence pointing to organised extortion networks operating behind alleged loan apps. Multiple complaints highlight prolonged harassment, data misuse, and intimidation of borrowers. Lack of awareness about data privacy, digital security further increases the risk, say police

title: Court’s interference in religious matters may break religions: SC
content: Senior advocate Raju Ramachandran says India is a civilisation governed by a Constitution; the courts should protect fundamental rights

title: Chhattisgarh HC acquits accused in 2010 Tadmetla encounter that left 76 security personnel dead
content: Court says it is distressed to observe a case of such a serious magnitude, involving mass casualties and grave consequences to national security, has no legally admissible and reliable evidence against the accused

title: Repatriation of illegal Bangladeshis is a ‘core issue’:  MEA response to Dhaka’s remarks on ‘push-in’ from India
content: Bangladesh puts border guards on alert, vows to take action if migrants are “pushed in” via the unguarded land border in Assam, West Bengal; no comment from MEA on Centre-State policy differences on this issue

title: Karnataka set to approach SC to implement MGNREGA for this year
content: We are already in May, and the Centre is yet to notify the new Act, frame rules and regulations. No alternative has been provided by the Centre: Patil

title: Governor’s remit is to invite TVK chief Vijay and ask for a floor test: former Law Minister Ashwani Kumar
content: The due process argument attributed to the Governor of Tamil Nadu is seen as a graceless stratagem on the part of the Governor to delay Vijay’s swearing-in despite a deafening popular mandate in his favour, he says

title: Kerala election results: Congress high command to pick Kerala CM; most UDF allies for Satheesan
content: A three-way fight is on in Kerala for the Chief Minister’s chair; outgoing Leader of Opposition V.D. Satheesan, Alappuzha MP K.C. Venugopal, and senior leader Ramesh Chennithala are in the race

title: Gurugram gated societies not letting in enumerators; FIRs recommended against enumerators obstructing Census work
content: Census Act, 1948 has provision for three years in jail for those found neglecting or obstructing Census duties; Gurugram Census officer writes to RWAs directing entry of Census workers with ID, authorisation letters

title: ‘No space, too old’: Four from Calvary Temple arrested for abandoning Siberian Huskies in Hyderabad outskirts
content: The Sangareddy police have written to GHMC seeking details regarding the licence held by the Calvary Temple management for dog breeding and maintenance activities.

title: Demographic imbalance in India needs more attention than population control, says RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat
content: With to Common Civil Code, Mr. Bhagwat referred to the introduction of the law in a few States in India, including Uttarakhand, and expressed optimism that the it will gradually be implemented across India

title: 'We’ll knock them out harder': Trump warns Iran after 3 US ships came under fire in Hormuz
content: President Donald Trump claimed three US destroyers were fired upon in the Strait of Hormuz but suffered no damage, stating Iranian attackers were destroyed. He issued a strong warning to Iran, threatening more violent military action if a deal is not signed quickly, calling the nation 'lunatics'.

title: 'Not all players are happy': Ex-India cricketer drops explosive claim on Gambhir
content: Former India cricketer Atul Wassan has raised concerns about the Indian team's dressing room atmosphere under head coach Gautam Gambhir. Wassan suggests that not all players are comfortable with Gambhir's intense and uncompromising style. He hinted that some cricketers feel sidelined despite the team's recent successes. This comes amid significant changes in the Indian squad, including retirements and captaincy shifts.

title: Big setback for Trump: US court rules 10% global tariffs unlawful
content: A US federal trade court ruled against President Trump's 10% global tariffs, deeming them invalid and unauthorized by law. The court found the administration's justification under a 1974 trade law insufficient, a setback for a key trade policy. This decision offers stability to businesses reliant on global supply chains.

title: Microsoft's first-ever voluntary retirement offer for 8,500-plus US employees: Here's what's in buyout package
content: Microsoft has unveiled its first voluntary retirement program, offering eligible US employees a package including five years of healthcare coverage (one fully paid), cash severance up to 39 weeks' pay, and accelerated stock vesting. This initiative aims to reduce headcount ahead of the new fiscal year, following recent layoffs and executive departures.

title: Betting, corruption allegations rock BPL, BCB suspends multiple individuals
content: In a statement, the BCB said the charges came after investigations carried out by the Bangladesh Cricket Board Integrity Unit (BCBIU). The probe looked into alleged corrupt conduct, betting activities, lack of cooperation in anti-corruption investigations and attempts to obstruct investigations linked to the BPL.

title: China should not have ... : Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang after share falls to zero in Beijing
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declared America a priority for advanced chip sales, stating China should not have access to the "latest and greatest" AI technology. However, he urged the US not to abandon the Chinese market entirely, arguing that global sales and increased tax revenues strengthen American economic and national security.

title: Will DMK support AIADMK to form govt in TN? MK Stalin to take final call
content: The legislators left the decision to Stalin, multiple sources told TOI. AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami, meanwhile, is understood to have sought governor R V Arlekar’s appointment on Friday.

title: Anthropic’s Boris Cherny says ‘manual coding is dead’ as Claude writes code
content: Anthropic's Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, says software engineering is already dead inside his own company—no one writes code by hand anymore, and Claude AI instances autonomously communicate over Slack to resolve problems across teams. As Google, Meta, Amazon and Snap race to set aggressive AI coding targets, Cherny believes IDE tools like VS Code and Xcode are next to disappear—and Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei's "centaur phase" warning suggests the window for human coders is closing fast.

title: Kyle Loftis death sparks questions months after major crash
content: Kyle Loftis, founder of 1320Video, died Tuesday night, according to the platform’s official statement. His cause of death has not been confirmed yet. Loftis helped build one of the biggest motorsports media brands online through street racing and automotive content. Fans, racers, and creators across social media continue sharing tributes and remembering his influence on modern car culture.

title: Simone Biles open up on therapy as Jonathan Owens eyes NFL return
content: Simone Biles opens up about her mental health struggles following the Tokyo Olympics, reflecting on the pressure that led her to step away from competition. In a recent interview, the Olympic champion shared how the experience changed her perspective on strength and vulnerability. Two years later, Biles continues to focus on her well-being while keeping her future in gymnastics open, including a possible return for the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

title: Joe Senser dies at 69 as unanswered questions surround final chapter of his life
content: Former Minnesota Vikings tight end Joe Senser has passed away at 69. Senser, a Pro Bowler and the only Vikings tight end to achieve over 1,000 receiving yards in a season, died on May 7th. The cause of death remains unknown, though he had been dealing with the aftermath of a severe stroke suffered in 2016.

title: Simone Biles, Jonathan Owens reveal lavish $3M Texas mansion at last after years
content: Simone Biles recently shared new poolside photos from her completed $3 million Texas mansion with Jonathan Owens. After years of planning and construction delays, the couple is finally enjoying their dream home together while Biles also continues thinking about her future ahead of the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics.

title: Nashik TCS case: Prime accused Nida Khan arrested after weeks on run
content: Nida Khan, an accused in the Nashik TCS case involving alleged sexual harassment and forced religious conversion, has been taken into custody from Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar. Khan was wanted after her anticipatory bail was rejected by the court. Police are continuing their investigation and legal proceedings.

title: Tannaz Irani opens up on marriage, divorce and bond with daughter
content: Tannaz Irani recently opened up about marriage, divorce, motherhood, guilt and healing relationships with children during a recent conversation. From talking about growing up in a progressive household to admitting she “could have handled things better” as a mother, the actress shared deeply personal moments from her life.

title: ‘Virtually no debate in Parliament’: SC hears plea challenging EC, CEC appointment law
content: The Supreme Court heard arguments challenging the 2023 law on appointing Election Commissioners, which replaced the CJI with a Union minister on the selection panel. Petitioners argued the law undermines the Election Commission's autonomy and was passed with minimal debate due to MP suspensions. The court questioned its power to direct Parliament on lawmaking.

title: 'I am healthy & happy': Shakti Kapoor breaks silence on fake death news; vows legal action
content: Veteran actor Shakti Kapoor has reacted strongly to false rumours about his death that recently surfaced on social media. The actor personally addressed the claims through a video message, assuring fans that he is absolutely fine and urging people not to spread misinformation online. Apart from clarifying that he is safe and healthy, Shakti Kapoor also revealed that he intends to take legal action against those responsible for spreading the fake news.

title: 'Humko bhi wicket chahiye': Dhoni bowls in nets amid wait over comeback - Watch
content: While fans continue to wait for his comeback, Dhoni was seen in a different role during a CSK training session on Thursday.

title: Stephen Curry, Ayesha mark major venture milestone after appearance
content: Stephen Curry, the Golden State Warriors’ star player, and his wife, Ayesha Curry, are pretty successful entrepreneurs even as they try to balance their busy professional lives along with their personal life and four children. While the high profile couple recently turned heads at the MET Gala, their recent venture has been pretty successful.

title: Operation Sindoor: Ten strategic lessons for India’s military future
content: Every conflict leaves behind more than tactical outcomes; it generates a blueprint for future preparedness. Operation Sindoor, when examined through a structural and doctrinal lens, reveals a decisive shift in how India must think about air power, attrition, industrial capacity, and integrated warfare. Its most enduring lessons lie not in what was achieved, but in what must now be built.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for May 8
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 8, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: 'This is not Covid': WHO assures Hantavirus outbreak 'limited' but more cases 'possible'
content: The World Health Organization has assured that a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is expected to be limited, though more cases are possible.  The rare Andes strain, which can spread between humans, is believed to have originated in Argentina.  Authorities confirm three passenger deaths and several infections globally, with diagnostic kits sent to affected nations.

title: Krunal Pandya, Pooran involved in heated on-field exchange - Watch
content: Lucknow Super Giants batter Nicholas Pooran and Royal Challengers Bengaluru all-rounder Krunal Pandya shared a brief on-field moment during a crucial IPL 2026 match. The interaction occurred after Pandya bowled a surprise bouncer to Pooran. Both players have a shared history at LSG. The match saw a high score from Mitchell Marsh.

title: Fact check: No proof Dianna Russini’s husband demanded DNA test
content: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini’s alleged affair rumors have only gone worse in the last few weeks as she had to step down from her successful career, sparking outrage among fans. While Mike Vrabel’s life remains unchanged, all focus is on the children that Dianna Russini and her husband, Kevin Goldschmidt, share together, as the popular coach gets dragged into it.

title: Father of child who died in boat tragedy says his son had a premonition
content: A 4-year-old boy, Trishan, reportedly showed unusual fear of water and expressed a premonition before the tragic Jabalpur boat accident on April 30, 2026. His father recounted Trishan's words, "Papa, we should not go into the water," and a chilling question asked during the cruise. The family's unpacked luggage serves as a painful reminder of the lives lost.

title: Bhuvneshwar Kumar scripts history, becomes first pacer to ...
content: Bhuvneshwar Kumar made history by becoming the first fast bowler to play 200 IPL matches, a feat he achieved with Royal Challengers Bengaluru. This milestone follows his earlier achievement of 200 IPL wickets. Kumar expressed pride in his accomplishment, highlighting the satisfaction of reaching this mark with a successful franchise.

title: Boney Kapoor, Janhvi Kapoor & Khushi Kapoor win legal battle over Sridevi’s Chennai property
content: In a major relief to producer Boney Kapoor and daughters Janhvi Kapoor and Khushi Kapoor, the Madras High Court has dismissed a civil suit filed over a Chennai property that was purchased in the name of late actress Sridevi in 1988. The case revolved around a 2.70-acre land parcel in Sholinganallur, Chennai. The plaintiffs had claimed to be the legal heirs of late MC Chandrasekaran and sought partition rights over the property.

title: From UK, BIS vaults to Indian shores: Why RBI wants to keep more & more gold at home
content: RBI is not alone in repatriating its gold reserves. Several central banks like France’s Banque de France, Germany’s Deutsche Bundesbank, Serbia’s National Bank have done the same - buy why?

title: Marsh tears into RCB with century, breaks Pant's record to become ...
content: Marsh reached the milestone in 49 balls, making it the fastest century by an LSG batter in IPL history. He went past Rishabh Pant’s 54-ball hundred against RCB at the same venue in IPL 2025.

title: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: "Will Knock Them Out A Lot Harder": Trump Threatens More Iran Strikes
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: The attacks shattered a period of relative calm in the Middle East since the ceasefire took effect on April 8.

title: Trump Threatens More Iran Strikes If Deal Not Signed "Fast"
content: "We'll knock them out a lot harder, and a lot more violently, in the future, if they don't get their Deal signed, FAST!", US President Donald Trump said as the US military carried out strikes on Iranian military targets Thursday.

title: US Urges Iran To Free Ailing Nobel Winner Activist Narges Mohammadi
content: The United States on Thursday urged Iran to free jailed Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi, who is believed to have had two heart attacks in recent weeks.

title: All 108 TVK MLAs To Resign If DMK, AIADMK Try To Form Government: Sources
content: The TVK, which won 108 seats -- two of them won by Vijay -- has 107 MLAs and argues that as the single-largest party, it should be invited by the Governor to form government.

title: Will DMK-Congress Split Rip Apart INDIA Bloc? UP Polls To Hold Clue
content: The Congress's move to ally with Vijay -- who has scooped up 108 seats in Tamil Nadu -- and get a chance of power sharing in the state has alienated the DMK

title: 2 Minors Suffocate To Death As Mother Tries To Set Herself On Fire In UP
content: Anita's daughters Vandna (10) and one-and-a-half-year-old Jasprit, who were inside the room at the time, were suffocated and fell unconscious.

title: US Military Strikes Iran's Qeshm Port, Bandar Abbas: Report
content: The US military on Thursday carried out strikes on Iran's Qeshm port and Bandar Abbas, a Fox News reporter said in a post on X, citing a senior US  official.

title: US Trade Court Rules Against Trump's 10% Global Tariffs
content: The US Court of International Trade ruled in favor of small businesses that challenged the tariffs, which took effect on February 24.

title: Trump Says Giving EU Until July 4 To Fulfill Trade Deal Or Will Raise Tariffs
content: Trump on Friday had threatened to increase tariffs on EU cars and trucks to 25% this week, from 15% currently, because the EU was not complying with the terms of a deal struck in Scotland last July.

title: 1 Year After Operation Sindoor, Children In Uri Still Carry Scars Of Shelling
content: While people are trying to rebuild their lives and hoping to see the end of hostilities on the border, the four-day war has left a deep impact on the psyche of children.

title: Former Calcutta High Court Chief Justice Resigns From SIR Tribunal
content: In his resignation letter, Justice Sivagnanam (retired) had cited personal reasons behind his decision.

title: 1 Year Of Op Sindoor: Target-By-Target Walkthrough, With Ex-IAF Vice Chief
content: Operation Sindoor: Air Marshal Narmdeshwar Tiwari (retired) discusses with NDTV's Vishnu Som a target-by-target walkthrough

title: AIADMK-DMK Pact Could Happen In Tamil Nadu, With Nudge From BJP: Sources
content: The rise of Vijay and his TVK may spark what has been considered impossible over the last 50 years - an alliance between Dravida arch-rivals DMK and AIADMK - all to keep the actor-politician out of power.

title: "Very Dynamic President": Trump Praises Meeting With Brazil's Lula At White House
content: US President Donald Trump said that he and Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva discussed many topics, including Trade and, specifically, Tariffs  at the White House on Thursday.

title: Tamil Nadu Election Results Highlights: Vijay-Governor Deadlock, New Possible Pacts In Tamil Nadu Thriller
content: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Live Updates: The TVK emerged as the single-largest party with 108 seats in the 234-member assembly, falling short of the majority mark of 118.

title: There's A Difference Between Circumcision, Genital Mutilation: Supreme Court
content: Siddharth Lutha, appearing for the petitioners opposing female genital mutilation, told the bench that the practice is done on young girls aged 7 years, and it causes an irreversible change in their bodies.

title: Ex-Trinamool MLA, Director Raj Chakraborty Quits Politics After Election Loss
content: Raj Chakraborty, who had won the Barrackpore seat in 2021, lost the constituency this time by 15,822 votes.

title: West Bengal: Governor dissolves assembly even as Mamata Banerjee refuses to quit
content: RN Ravi dissolved the assembly as the election process for a new legislature concluded . Mamata Banerjee maintained she wouldn’t resign as CM . the governor’s decision coincided with an election commission move to lift the model code of conduct that had been in force since midMarch .

title: Over 40 India-bound vessels stuck amid Hormuz deadlock
content: Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. Read More

title: Snake in mid-day meal? Over 150 students fall ill after lunch at Bihar school
content: Adwitiya primarily covers crime and civic stories. She has in the past written on varied topics, including decline of the glass bangle culture, illegal trade of firecrackers in Champahati, West Bengal apart from many more on food and travel. She has recently completed her PGDM in journalism from Xavier's, Mumbai. She loves music and is very affectionate about animals. Read More

title: Nepal opposition wants PM Balendra Shah to hold Lipulekh talks with India
content: the demand was raised at the house of representatives’ international relations and tourism committee in Singha Durbar amid fresh unease over India and China using the Lipulekh route for trade and Kailash Mansarovar Yatra without Nepal’s consent . Nepali Congress MP Sandip Rana said Nepal should not delay direct “table talks” with both neighbours .

title: ‘India steadfast in defeating terrorism’: PM Modi on Op Sindoor anniversary
content: PM Modi says India remains steadfast as ever in its resolve to defeat terrorism and destroy its enabling ecosystem . he changed display pictures (DP) on his social media handles to an image of Operation Sindoor as a mark of respect to armed forces . the entire nation salutes our forces for their valour .

title: All eyes on Amit Shah as West Bengal waits for new CM and cabinet
content: Rohit Khanna covers sectors like government finance, economy, industry and issues related to financial crime.

title: Former CJI DY Chandrachud to mediate in Kapurs’ dispute
content: former chief justice of india D Y Chandrachud has been roped in to settle the high-profile property dispute between late industrialist Sunjay Kapur's mother and his wife . the family trust tied to his Rs 30,000 crore estate is tied to the late industrialistic's family trust . a bench of justices told the parties to explore an amicable settlement .

title: Did Vijayan’s over-projection prove its undoing? CPM begins analysis of Kerala debacle
content: the party is holding a preliminary review of the results on Wednesday and Thursday . the result assessment at the Politburo and central committee meetings will look at every factor ranging from organisational gaps to functioning of the govt . there is no place within it for "cult politics" and the campaign was not a one-man show .

title: Somnath and Bharat’s unconquerable spirit!
content: in less than half a year, it is a privilege to be attending two important milestones relating to Somnath and its journey from ruin to renewal . the vast sea before it evokes timelessness. the waves tell us that no matter how fierce the storms are, one can always rise again with dignity and strength .

title: Bangladesh seeks China support for Teesta restoration project
content: the govt has sought China’s “involvement and support” for the Teesta restoration project . the move risks clouding New Delhi-Dhaka relations . many in Bangladesh hope that with BJP, which is at the Centre, soon to form governor in Bengal, there is an opportunity to revisit the treaty .

title: Hitmen hired to kill Suvendu’s aide conducted recce for 72 hours: SIT
content: the assailants fired eight rounds from Glock 47X semi-automatic pistols . three bullets each struck Rath, 38, and the person at the wheel of his SUV, Buddhadeb Bera . a third person in the rear seat, Mintu, escaped by ducking .

title: ‘Family vomited major toxin’: How rat poison inside watermelon led to death of 4 in Mumbai
content: forensic laboratories in Kalina have found rat poison in the watermelon killed four members of the Dokadia family . zinc phosphide (rat poison) was found in the fruit as well as the viscera (internal organs) it is unlikely to have entered the fruit through an injection, official says .
